summary: sensivity ships hidden Windows persistence, obfuscated eval payload, and YouTube-gated activation
details: On load, launcher.js installs an HKCU Run key named 'OneDriveUpdate' that points at a sibling VBS file (OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs) which silently spawns `node.exe launcher.js` with hidden window style. The package then forks a detached supervisor process whose title is set to 'Runtime Broker' (a real Windows DCOM component name) and which respawns the worker on every exit, exiting only when a `.sensivity-stop` sentinel file appears. Inside the supervised worker, launcher.js executes `eval(fs.readFileSync('server.obf.js'))` against a 149KB heavily obfuscated bundle (obfuscator.io, string array length 724 with rotate + decoder), so reviewers and scanners cannot see what the runtime payload actually does. The package also bundles a 6.8MB Windows native module sens.node containing GTA V / RAGE-engine cheat strings ('Freecam', 'Teleport', 'Waypoint', 'spawnVehicle', '__licenseAccepted'), which is unrelated to the package's self-description as 'Sensivity Control Panel'. launcher.js further uses PowerShell UI Automation to enumerate Chrome/Edge/Opera/Brave windows every 3 seconds and only reveals its panel-access QR when the user is viewing YouTube video ID `wJWta2lO0Lw` — an environment-keyed activation gate that hides the package's behavior from anyone not carrying the trigger. Names ('OneDriveUpdate', 'OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs', 'Runtime Broker') are chosen to impersonate Microsoft components and evade Task Manager / autostart inspection. Installer harm: persistent hidden execution of attacker-controlled obfuscated code on Windows hosts, with respawn-on-exit and Run-key reinstatement on every launch.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
